key_insight: A static 5D PCYS interpolation table, periodically recalibrated with first-order linearization, can approximate costly VPSC-based irradiation-induced deformation in zirconium alloy components with high accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical multi-scale FEM simulations of nuclear core internals, such as spacer grids and cladding, with reduced computational cost while retaining microstructural fidelity.
method: Embed a 5D interpolation table as a surrogate material law in Code_Aster FEM.
Use periodic recalibration plus first-order Taylor linearization to capture irradiation-driven microstructural drift.
Benchmark the surrogate against coupled VPSC-FEM simulations under continuous high-dose irradiation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports relative errors below 1% for representative deformation paths and notes meaningful computational savings in spacer grid simulations.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the validity of periodic recalibration and the deformation paths used to build the interpolation tables, so extrapolation beyond the trained irradiation regime may be risky.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
